---
title: Work
---

A showcase of projects focused on environmental science, public-sector data systems, and reproducible analysis.

## Relevant Project Experience

- Our Freshwater 2026 - Internal Consistency Checking — Ministry for the Environment publication support; Environmental reporting, QA, data assurance

	- Contributed to internal consistency checking of _Our Freshwater 2026_ report before publication.
	- Supported quality assurance of environmental evidence used to inform national and regional policy discussions.
	- Link: https://environment.govt.nz/publications/our-freshwater-2026/

- National Indicators - River and Lake Water Quality — Stats NZ Environmental Reporting team collaboration; R, indicator data, publication workflows

	- Collaborated with analysts in the Environmental Reporting team to publish national indicator data for river and lake water quality.
	- Helped maintain reliable and transparent data pipelines that support environmental decision-making.
	- Link: https://www.stats.govt.nz/indicators/river-water-quality-nitrogen-data-to-2024/

- Ngā Tūtōhu Aotearoa - Indicators Aotearoa New Zealand — Technical lead for environmental and wellbeing indicators platform; R Shiny, data products, stakeholder communication

	- Led technical development of the Indicators Aotearoa New Zealand platform, integrating diverse environmental datasets into accessible reporting tools.
	- Worked with internal and external users to translate complex data needs into practical, customer-focused outputs.
	- Link: https://statisticsnz.shinyapps.io/wellbeingindicators/

- Customer-Centred Data Delivery Across Environmental Roles — Stats NZ and Earth Sciences New Zealand (formerly NIWA); Stakeholder engagement, service delivery, communication

	- Delivered data products and support to a wide range of stakeholders and customers in both public sector and science service settings.
	- Built strong working relationships through clear communication, responsiveness, and practical problem solving.
	- Mentored analysts and early career staff across Stats NZ and Earth Sciences New Zealand. Earlier on as a Biology Professor, supporting capability growth, confidence, and collaborative team performance.


## Predicting Macroalgal Introductions at Global and Regional Scales

- Period: Dec 2015 - Nov 2019
- Status: Completed
- Technologies: LaTeX, R, Git, Machine Learning, GIS
- Description: Doctoral thesis focused on predicting the introduction and spread of invasive macroalgal species using machine learning and spatial analysis.

Highlights:

- Predictive models using Random Forest and MaxEnt for invasion hotspots.
- Analysis of environmental and biological drivers.
- Peer-reviewed publications and conference presentations.
- Contributions to open-source ecological modeling workflows.

Links:

- [GitHub](https://github.com/nrob536/doctoral-thesis)

## Git Learning Environment

- Period: August 2024
- Status: Current
- Technologies: Git, shell scripting, R
- Description: A repository to test Git skills through unsupervised challenges, from beginner to expert levels, set in a magical fantasy world.

Highlights:

- Immersive storylines and characters for each challenge.
- Tasks covering branching, merging, rebasing, and conflict resolution.
- Automated feedback and hints for learners.
- Open-sourced design for community contributions.

Links:

- [GitHub](https://github.com/nrob536/git-fantasy-quest)

## Interactive Population Clock (Demo)

- Period: September 2025
- Status: Current
- Technologies: R plumber, REST API, Docker, JavaScript, HTML, CSS
- Description: Interactive Population Clock built with Plumber and deployed with Docker.

Highlights:

- Real-time population REST API implementation.
- Containerized deployment for reproducibility.
- Cloud-hosted deployment on Render.
- Interactive front-end experience.

Links:

- [GitHub](https://github.com/nrob536/popclock-api)
- [Live demo](https://nrob536.github.io/popclock-api/)
